source.definition: in exactly the same way or to the same extent

explanation.sourceText: The phrase "just as" combines the adverb "just," meaning exactly or precisely, with the conjunction "as," used for comparisons, to express equality or similarity in manner or degree.
explanation.targetText: Expresia engleză „just as” combină adverbul „just”, care înseamnă exact sau precis, cu conjuncția „as”, folosită pentru comparații, pentru a exprima egalitate sau similitudine în mod sau măsură.

source.additional: It is used to emphasize equality or similarity between two elements.

target.definition: în același mod sau în aceeași măsură

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian phrase "la fel" is an internal formation from the Romanian words "la" (at/by) and "fel" (kind, manner), meaning literally "in the same way."
explanation.targetText: Expresia românească „la fel” este formată intern din cuvintele „la” și „fel”, însemnând literal „la mod” sau „în același fel”.

target.additional: Kann in alltäglichen Gesprächen verwendet werden, wenn man Ähnlichkeiten feststellt.

examples.sourceText: The weather today is just as beautiful as yesterday.
examples.targetText: Vremea este la fel de frumoasă ca ieri.

examples.sourceText: She feels just as exhausted as I do.
examples.targetText: Se simte la fel de obosită ca și mine.

learningTips.text: Think of "la fel" like identical twins who look exactly the same – a perfect image for equality.
Remember "la fel" as a bridge connecting two different ideas by their similarity.
Imagine "just as" as a mirror reflecting two identical things – this helps you recall "la fel."
When you say "just as," associate it with the idea that two things have equal value or degree.
